_comment: Preflight data for Fly.io vs AWS infra comparison eval. Captured on 2026-04-19 from live service describe + curl probes. See REPORT.md §2.

db_pool_config.note: Same defaults as Fly. Expect pool exhaustion at same concurrency ceiling.

measurement_client.note: Both Fly (sjc) and AWS (us-west-2) are on the US west coast. AWS (Oregon) is geographically closer to YVR than Fly's sjc region, but this does not fully account for the observed 3.5x TTFB gap.

baseline_probes.description: n=3 warm samples after cold boot to verify connectivity before full suite

both_platforms_share.frontend_bundle: same Docker image; Vite build identical (verified 868 byte root HTML on both)

auth_mode.impact: /internal/reset-dev-profile fails on Fly in prod mode. Mitigation: set SGA_EVAL_RESET_PROFILE=0 for Test D so latency measurement is unaffected.

cpu_architecture.impact: Architecture divergence is real and may partly explain compute latency differences. ARM64 Fargate chosen in PR #147 because Tailwind v4 oxide amd64 binary produced broken CSS bundle.

cold_start.impact: Test C dropped from bundle; documented as design trade-off in REPORT.md topology section.

edge_layer.fly: Fly Anycast (TLS terminates at nearest PoP)

scope_changes_from_original_plan: Test C (cold start) dropped — AWS desired_count=1 has no cold start; design trade-off documented in topology section instead of being measured.
Test E (throughput knee) deferred — RDS pool config on AWS is identical to Fly (pool_size=5+10 overflow), so running 20u on AWS risks wedging it the same way it wedged Fly. If run at all, cap at 10u.
Chat endpoint corrected to POST /session/{session_id}/chat (not POST /chat as originally planned).
SGA_EVAL_RESET_PROFILE=0 required for Test D on Fly (prod auth mode).
